checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present including customSections for budget config and Notion schema

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows (daily collection 15 edges, monthly report 11 edges, error recovery 11 edges) — all properly connected

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: Daily 7AM schedule and monthly 1st-of-month 8AM schedule — both valid crons

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: openai connector has role 'ai_platform' with no builtin definition. However, template uses http_request with API key, making it self-contained.

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 4 adoption_questions added covering Slack channel, Notion DB, daily budget, and monthly budget

checks.name: default_value_coverage
checks.detail: Budget defaults ($15/day, $350/month) and Slack channel default provided

checks.name: prompt_quality
checks.detail: Identity 62w, instructions 700w+ with daily and monthly sections, tool guidance with OpenAI/Notion/Slack API endpoints, 3 examples, comprehensive error handling

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. Read-only against OpenAI usage API.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 62w (>50), instructions cover daily collection (8 steps) and monthly reporting (7 steps) totaling 700+ words, tool guidance with specific OpenAI Usage API endpoints, 3 examples (normal, exceeded, monthly), comprehensive error handling per API.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request for all 3 APIs with endpoint documentation. file_read/file_write for local state. All tools referenced in prompt match suggested_tools. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Daily 7AM for usage collection and monthly 1st-at-8AM for optimization report. Both schedules align precisely with the described workflows and timing.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Notion and Slack have builtins. OpenAI connector is well-defined with credential fields but has no builtin — template is still functional via http_request with API key.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Local state tracks budget thresholds, rolling averages, and last run. Monthly cache avoids re-querying Notion. Agent memory stores trends and optimization insights. Good design.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Per-API error handling: OpenAI (401, 429, 5xx, empty data), Notion (400, 404, 429), Slack (channel_not_found, invalid_auth). Data anomaly detection prevents false alerts. Credential refresh flow.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: 3 flows cover daily collection with budget evaluation, monthly report with optimization recommendations, and error recovery with backfill. All prompt steps map to flow nodes.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Unmistakable value: automated tracking and budget enforcement for AI API spending with monthly optimization recommendations. Directly saves money.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: OpenAI Usage API requires Organization Owner/Reader role and 'Usage: Read' permission. Notion and Slack have builtins. OpenAI lacks a builtin connector but works via http_request.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Tiered budget alerting, per-model cost analysis, model downgrade recommendations, caching opportunity detection, and batch processing suggestions go well beyond simple cost tracking.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 4 adoption questions are necessary. Budget amounts and Notion DB ID are required for core functionality. Slack channel customizes delivery.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for budgets ($15/day, $350/month) and Slack channel (#ai-cost-alerts). Notion DB ID requires user input. Template works immediately with defaults for cost tracking.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — connectors need user-specific configuration
OpenAI connector has no builtin definition — requires user to set up API key via connector form
OpenAI Usage API requires Organization-level permissions which may not be available to all users

template: Added 4 adoption_questions: slack_alert_channel, notion_tracking_db, daily_budget, monthly_budget
